article_title: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse Goes Open Source Under MIT, Six Weeks After Microsoft Signaled the Move
summary: All facts verified across sources; allowlist warnings are policy notes only — no factual issues found

content_preview.title: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse Goes Open Source Under MIT, Six Weeks After Microsoft Signaled the Move
content_preview.summary: Microsoft open-sourced its Eclipse IDE Copilot plugin on May 21, releasing roughly 15,000 lines of Java under MIT and drawing 1,200+ stars within hours.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft has open-sourced the GitHub Copilot plugin for Eclipse, releasing the full client-side Java codebase under the MIT license. The move, announced officially via the [GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-05-21-github-copilot-for-eclipse-is-open-source/) on May 21, 2026, was telegraphed six weeks earlier when Jialuo Gan, a Program Manager at Microsoft, [wrote on the Microsoft for Java Developers blog](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/java/ghc-eclipse-is-going-ope...

recommendations: Consider adding marketplace.eclipse.org and windowsnews.ai to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid developer news article covering the GitHub Copilot for Eclipse open-sourcing. Good structure covering what was released, what wasn't, context, community reception, and installation stats. 706 words fits News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (GitHub Changelog): confirmed May 21 announcement, MIT license, all 5 contributor names (@iloveeclipse @travkin79 @rsd-darshan @arpitjain099 @raghucssit), 'inviting the community to explore' quote verbatim. source-1.html.gz (Microsoft Java blog): confirmed April 8 pre-announcement date, Jialuo Gan author, 'in the coming weeks' quote, subscription requirement. source-2.html.gz (GitHub repository): confirmed version 0.18.0 tagged May 20 2026, 99.3% Java, usage-based billing reference. source-3.html.gz (WindowsNews): confirmed 15,000 lines of Java, 1,200+ stars and 90 forks within hours, 30% latency reduction via Jetty, Ollama fork, SQLite plaintext cache, telemetry behavior, VS Code (2024), JetBrains/Visual Studio (2025) timeline. source-4.html.gz (Eclipse Marketplace): confirmed 1/605 ranking and 15,649 installations.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key facts verified across available source snapshots. Technical details (99.3% Java, version 0.18.0, contributor names, installation counts) all verified. No fabrications detected.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GitHub Copilot for Eclipse articles in archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, well-sourced developer news article. APPROVE.
